headlines.title: Watch live: Emergency crews respond to brush fire in Palo Alto
headlines.description: Emergency crews are responding Monday to a brush fire in Palo Alto. The blaze is reported in the area of Arastradero Road and Caballo Lane, just northwest of Palo Alto Hills Golf & Country Club. Authorities report the fire has burned several acres with a slow rate of spread. No other information was immediately available.

headlines.title: San Francisco home with a history of squatters hits the market for $1.3 million
headlines.description: An abandoned house near San Francisco’s Castro neighborhood that has been popular with squatters for years is now for sale. On Yukon Street at the edge of Kite Hill in the Eureka Valley neighborhood, the house with arched windows over the garage, including one that’s broken, is listed for $1.3 million. Listing agent Zara Rowbotham and her brother, James, put together a promotional video highlighting the home’s fixer-upper potential. There is no running water or power at the house. Neighbors have reported to the city that squatters relieve themselves at the top floor atrium. “They needed a place to do it, so they had the nice manners to do it in one basket,” Rowbotham said. “Unfortunately it was an outside basket right in front of one of the neighbors’ houses.” With the nature of San Francisco’s red-hot housing market, Rowbothom said they already have a potential buyer. Rowbothom added the city is swirling with money right now and there are few places to buy, so properties like the one on Yukon Street – even with a history of squatters – are being snapped up quickly. Rowbothom said they’re going for millions of dollars, with people paying cash a lot of the time.

headlines.title: World Dog Surfing Championships in Pacifica to celebrate 10-year anniversary
headlines.description: The World Dog Surfing Championships is set to arrive in Pacifica for its 10-year anniversary this summer. Presented by Lagunitas Brewing Company and other premier sponsors, the competition will take place on Linda Mar Beach, also known as Pacifica State Beach. The free events will be held on July 31 and August 1. The main surfing event lasts from 9 a.m. to 2 p.m. on August 1, beginning with dog check ins and ending with an awards ceremony. Animals Mar 31 Animals Aug 2, 2025 Animals Aug 4, 2024 No tickets are required to view the dog surfing competition, but registration is required for dogs participating. While the event is free, there are opportunities to donate to partnered charities or to donate to a team entered in the dog surfing competition. Other events include a pet wellness fair, mobile pet adoptions, costume contests and a “yappy hour” afterwards. To learn more about the breakdown of events, visit surfdogchampionships.com/events.

headlines.title: WalletHub ranks best and worst states for summer road trips
headlines.description: California is known by many for its stunning views, seasonal activities and likeable climate; all features that could add to a positive summer road trip. However, there are many other factors which contribute to the difficulty of traveling within the state. WalletHub ranked California at 47th place in its Best & Worst States for Summer Road Trips (2026) ranking. The state landed in first place for its activities and second for its scenic views, but fell short in other categories. California placed 50th under costs and 43rd under safety, tanking its overall grade. WalletHub broke down cost into subcategories that further explain why California landed toward the bottom: For average gas prices, California placed 50th. For average cost of car repairs, California placed 48th. For price of camping, California placed 48th. California’s total score under WalletHub’s ranking added to 46.56, whereas Minnesota ended up at 61.11, the highest of all 50 states. Review the full report at wallethub.com. Real Estate Jun 18 Bay Area Housing Jun 17 Making It in the Bay Aug 27, 2025 1-bedroom apartment now costs over $3,400 a month to rent in San Francisco

headlines.title: Public transportation guide for San Francisco's Pride parade and celebrations
headlines.description: San Francisco’s annual Pride celebration, one of the largest in the world, will kick off during the last weekend of June and transit agencies are ready to help make the experience as smooth as possible. The theme of the 56th annual San Francisco Pride will be “Resistance in Action.” The fair will be located at Civic Center Plaza on Saturday and Sunday, and the parade will march down Market Street on Sunday. The parade will begin at 10:30 a.m. at Embarcadero Plaza and will travel down Market Street before finishing at City Hall. Spectators can watch along the entire 1.6-mile-long route. BART has four stops along the parade route — Embarcadero, Montgomery, Powell and Civic Center stations. San Francisco 21 hours ago Giants News Jun 19 San Francisco Jun 5 Saturday will have normal service, but on Sunday the agency plans to help people “ride with pride” by opening at 8 a.m. and offering five-line service. Trains will be scheduled to arrive every five minutes at the downtown stations. After 9 p.m., service will be reduced to three lines. Special event trains will be run between Millbrae and downtown San Francisco, as well as from Pleasant Hill from 9:30 a.m. until 11 a.m., before restarting at 2 p.m. until the evening. Muni will be rerouting due to regular travel down Market Street onto Mission Street. Muni Metro light-rail vehicles will remain in service, allowing attendees to enter and exit at Embarcadero, Montgomery, Powell and Civic Center stations. Caltrain will be offering its half-hourly regular weekend service from 9:30 a.m. to 1 p.m. Riders can then transfer onto a Muni Metro line after arriving in the Downtown San Francisco Fourth and King station. The San Francisco Bay Ferry will be offering additional morning service from Vallejo, Oakland and Alameda alongside their regular weekend service. Riders traveling from Richmond, Alameda or Oakland are offered free parking at ferry-affiliated structures. For those traveling on buses, Alameda-Contra Costa Transit will be offering its regular weekend transbay services to the Salesforce Transit Center. Golden Gate Transit will be operating its buses and ferries on its regular weekend service. Those looking to arrive early are encouraged to take the 9 a.m. Larkspur ferry that will travel directly to the San Francisco Ferry Terminal. More information on the Pride celebration can be found at sfpride.org
headlines.source: Andres Jimenez Larios | Bay City News and Sophia Taylor

headlines.title: Adobe's third semaphore puzzle solved in San Jose after three years
headlines.description: One of the world’s most difficult puzzles created has been solved after three years. Brian Vincent, a San Jose software engineer, was able to crack the code for the third Adobe Semaphore puzzle, the tech giant announced. Vincent solved it this past spring after Adobe launched the creation in May 2023. The first puzzle was displayed in 2006, created by Ben Rubin, an artist who heavily incorporates science and technology into his work. Containing four large LED wheels that rotate and land at different axis positions, the puzzle draws audiences and can take years to solve. The first puzzle was solved in 2007 and the second in 2017. A fourth puzzle is in the making and will be open for anyone to attempt to solve. To hear more about Vincent’s solving strategies and Rubin’s creation of the puzzle, visit adobe.com/sj-semaphore.

headlines.title: World Cup in the Bay Area: Jordan vs. Algeria in Santa Clara
headlines.description: World Cup action returns to the Bay Area Monday night when Jordan takes on Algeria in group stage play. Kickoff for the Group J match at San Francisco Bay Area Stadium is scheduled for 8 p.m. Both nations came up short in their World Cup openers last Tuesday. Jordan fell to Austria, 3-1, while Algeria was blanked by Argentina, 3-0. Monday night’s clash will mark the fourth World Cup match at the South Bay stadium. One more group stage match – Paraguay vs. Australia – is scheduled for Thursday, followed by a Round of 32 contest featuring the United States on July 1. San Jose 21 hours ago 2026 World Cup Jun 17 San Jose Jun 20 Organizers to add 4th screen in San Pedro Square for World Cup watch parties

headlines.title: Person hospitalized after possibly struck by BART at Walnut Creek station
headlines.description: A person who may have been hit by a train Monday morning at BART’s Walnut Creek station has been hospitalized, officials said. The station was initially closed after the incident about 10:45 a.m. but has since reopened. The person reportedly entered the trackway as a train approached at and may have been hit, a BART spokesperson said. The injured person was taken to a hospital. Their condition was not released. This is a developing story. Please check back for updates.

headlines.title: California files lawsuit against Trump administration over Clean Air Act
headlines.description: California Attorney General Rob Bonta announced Monday that the state has filed a lawsuit against the Trump administration over its decision to reclassify four Clean Air Act preemption waivers. The waivers include the 2008 greenhouse gas emission standards waiver, the Advanced Clean Cars I (ACC I) waiver, the 2022 reinstatement of portions of the ACC I waiver and the 2022 Small Off-Road Engine rule amendment. The waivers allow California to implement emissions standards that are stricter than federal requirements. The Environmental Protection Agency originally granted the waivers as “orders.” The agency is now reclassifying those orders as “rules.” State officials argue the change carries significant consequences. “This may sound very technical, and it is, but it’s also a critical distinction. Orders aren’t subject to expedited congressional disapproval,” Bonta said. “Rules, however, are subject to expedited congressional disapproval.” Bonta argues the EPA lacks the legal authority to reclassify the waivers and is exceeding its authority in what he described as an effort to undermine California’s clean-air policies and environmental progress.

headlines.title: Bay Area temperatures expected to rise through midweek, ocean remains hazardous
headlines.description: A warming trend will build across the Bay Area and Central Coast through midweek, while dangerous beach conditions are expected to persist through Wednesday afternoon, according to the National Weather Service. Temps are expected to gradually climb through the middle of the week, with the warmest conditions arriving on Wednesday. Pockets of Moderate HeatRisk are possible in the Santa Clara Valley, where highs could reach the 80s and lower 90s. The risk is greatest for people sensitive to heat, including older adults, children, people with health conditions and those without adequate cooling or hydration, forecasters said Monday. While inland temps rise, the weather service is also warning beachgoers about a long-period southerly swell that is increasing the risk of sneaker waves and strong rip currents along the Pacific coast. The greatest threat is expected at south- and southwest-facing beaches from the North Bay to Monterey County. Forecasters urged visitors to stay off jetties, piers and shoreline rocks, and reminded anyone heading to the coast to never turn their back on the ocean. The Beach Hazards Statement remains in effect through Wednesday afternoon.

headlines.title: San Jose considers extending parking meter hours, doubling rates downtown
headlines.description: A night out in downtown San Jose could soon cost drivers more. The city on Tuesday will vote on a proposal that would extend parking meter hours and increase parking rates as officials look to capitalize on increased nightlife activity downtown. Under the proposal, metered parking would be enforced until 9 p.m. instead of 6 p.m. The hourly rate would also double from $2 to $4. City officials say about 1,600 parking meters would be affected. Some meters, including those in Japantown and east of San Jose City Hall, would be exempt from the changes. San Jose 7 hours ago San Jose 5 hours ago San Jose Jun 19 The proposed $4 hourly rate would match the cost of parking in several city-owned garages. San Jose’s smart meters allow drivers to pay by tapping a card or paying through a mobile app, eliminating the need to carry coins or cash. The city estimates extending meter hours and increasing rates could generate about $1.2 million in additional annual revenue. San Jose has not raised parking meter rates downtown since 2014, officials said. If the council approves the rate resolution, the increase would likely go into effect in August after the meters are reprogrammed.

headlines.title: Man dies after San Jose e-scooter crash, police say
headlines.description: A man injured in an electric scooter crash in San Jose has died, marking the city’s 22nd traffic-related fatality of 2026, police said. The crash occurred at about 4:56 p.m. June 9 near Commercial Street and Oakland Road, San Jose police noted. According to a preliminary investigation by the San Jose Police Department, the rider was traveling eastbound in a westbound bike lane on a black Segway electric scooter when he lost control at a high rate of speed and fell. Police said the rider was not wearing a helmet. He was taken to a local hospital with life-threatening injuries and later stabilized. Investigators learned Tuesday that the man died from his injuries on June 13, police said. The Santa Clara County Medical Examiner’s Office will release the victim’s identity. Anyone with information about the crash is asked to contact the Detective Banzon of the San Jose Police Department’s Traffic Investigations Unit at: 4661@sanjoseca.gov or (408) 277-4654.
